items.title: Anthropic opened its Mythos-class model
items.oneliner: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5 publicly and claims 72.9% on CursorBench, raising the floor for production coding agents.

items.title: Apple pushed Apple Intelligence deeper into iOS
items.oneliner: Apple used WWDC to make Siri more conversational, signaling on-device AI is becoming a platform feature, not an app.

items.title: React Compiler is moving to Rust
items.oneliner: Meta's experimental Rust port runs about 3x faster than the Babel plugin, which could make compiler-driven React practical.

items.title: Germany made Google own AI answers
items.oneliner: A German court said AI Overviews are Google's own words, putting legal liability directly on generated search answers.

items.title: npm v12 will stop auto-running scripts
items.oneliner: npm v12 turns install scripts off by default and blocks Git dependencies, finally treating package install as code execution.

items.title: Anthropic broadened law enforcement disclosures
items.oneliner: Anthropic's privacy update gives it wider discretion to share data with law enforcement, raising enterprise governance questions.

items.title: Apple documented macOS container machines
items.oneliner: Apple's container-machine docs point to a more native macOS container story, which matters for local AI and CI workflows.

items.title: LaunchDarkly added runtime controls for agents
items.oneliner: LaunchDarkly wants AI agents managed like feature flags, giving teams live behavior control without redeploying model code.

items.title: Free AI infrastructure came with a bill
items.oneliner: A startup says Blacksmith's free trial quietly ran past limits and produced a $1,000 invoice, so AI tooling costs need guardrails.

items.title: PostgreSQL added property graphs
items.oneliner: Postgres 19 adds property graphs as read-only views, bringing graph queries closer to mainstream operational databases.
